title: karpathy-autoresearch
summary: This repo understands that the most powerful research methodology is the simplest one: give an AI agent one file to edit, one metric to optimize, one time budget, and an infinite loop.  The program.
creator_view: This repo understands that the most powerful research methodology is the simplest one: give an AI agent one file to edit, one metric to optimize, one time budget, and an infinite loop. The program.md is an entire research organization compressed into 114 lines.

candidates.name: program.md Agent Instruction Pattern
candidates.description: 114-line autonomous research protocol: setup, experiment loop, keep/discard, crash recovery, NEVER STOP, simplicity criterion. Compare against SKILL.md format.

candidates.name: Fixed-Budget Experimentation
candidates.description: Fixed 5-min time budget makes all experiments comparable. ~12/hour, ~100 overnight. Apply to any time-bounded agent work.

candidates.name: 3-File Architecture (Immutable/Editable/Instructions)
candidates.description: prepare.py (don't touch) + train.py (agent edits) + program.md (human edits). Clean editable-zone contract.

candidates.name: Autonomous Crash Recovery Protocol
candidates.description: Read stack trace, attempt fix, revert if unfixable. Simpler than checkpoint+resume for low-cost tasks.

candidates.name: Results TSV Logging Convention
candidates.description: commit/metric/memory/status/description. Already have JSONL equivalent. Low extraction value.

candidates.name: Simplicity Criterion for JASON-OS
candidates.description: 'Removing something and getting equal or better results is a great outcome.' Growth discipline for 72-skill ecosystem.

candidates.name: Protocol vs Workflow Design Space
candidates.description: program.md (protocol: one agent, one metric, infinite loop) vs SKILL.md (workflow: multi-agent, multi-phase, convergence). When to use which?

candidates.name: Autonomy Spectrum Design
candidates.description: NEVER STOP (autoresearch) vs explicit approval gates (CLAUDE.md #2). JASON-OS needs: autonomous within phases, gated between phases.

candidates.name: analysis.ipynb Results Methodology
candidates.description: Framework: load TSV, keep/discard rates, progress plot with running minimum, rank improvements by delta. Apply to review-metrics.jsonl, learning-routes.jsonl.

candidates.name: Hidden Multi-Agent Architecture
candidates.description: .gitignore: worktrees/ + queue/ + generated CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. Multi-agent infrastructure exists but isn't shared. Mirrors your worktree usage.

candidates.name: No-License-on-Purpose Trap
candidates.description: Karpathy can get away with no license. You can't. Always include LICENSE if publishing JASON-OS.

candidates.name: Single-Metric Optimization Trap
candidates.description: val_bpb works because the problem has one metric. Don't flatten multi-objective problems into one number.

security.note: No license. No input validation. GPU code with shell commands. Designed for trusted single-user.

reliability.note: No tests. No CI. Crash recovery is agent-level, not code-level.

maintainability.note: 3 files, ~1200 lines. Radically simple by design.

process.note: No license, no releases, no CI, no tests. Intentionally minimal.

velocity.note: 31 days old, 67K stars, 1 commit recently. Ship-and-stabilize.

absencePattern.description: Missing license, tests, CI, releases are intentional design choices. The repo is a research artifact, not a library.

creatorLens: This repo understands that the most powerful research methodology is the simplest one: give an AI agent one file to edit, one metric to optimize, one time budget, and an infinite loop. The program.md is an entire research organization compressed into 114 lines.
